turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court this morning in two cases.

turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: The first is Alexander versus the SC Conference of the NAACP, 22-807. The 2020 census required South Carolina to redraw its congressional map. The plaintiffs in this case challenged the map on the ground that it was a racial gerrymander. The District Court agreed and enjoined the state from using the map. In today’s opinion we hold that the District Court committed clear error when it found a racial gerrymandering. For that reason, we reverse the judgment of the District Court in part and remand for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. The opinion of the Court is joined in full by the Chief Justice and by Justices Gorsuch, Kavanaugh and Barrett. Justice Thomas has joined all but part 3C, and Justice Thomas has filed an opinion concurring in part. Justice Kagan has filed a dissenting opinion joined in full by Justices Sotomayor and Jackson.
